title: sIATC statement file
description: Schema for a .sIATC.json file produced by sIATC — Statement of Intellectual Fellowship and Accountability for Content. A single-author self-assessment of human/AI interaction across nine content-production stages. sIATC is a derivative of the sIfA Tool (Schomerus / Busara), Apache-2.0.

tool.description: Informational. The tool that wrote the file, e.g. 'sIATC'.

basedOn.description: Informational provenance note crediting the upstream sIfA tool.

version.description: Schema major version. Bumped only on incompatible changes.

kind.description: Discriminator marking this as a sIATC statement.

title.description: Title of the piece the statement describes.

author.description: Author/owner who takes responsibility for the content.

stages.description: One entry per content stage. A full sIATC has all nine; smaller fixtures stay valid.

applicable.description: False when the stage did not occur for this piece (e.g. a monolingual piece has no Translation). Non-applicable stages are excluded from the figure; extent must then be 0.

extent.description: Extent of AI use in this stage. 0 = none (a human did it); 1 = some; 2 = extensive.

tool.description: AI tool or service used in this stage (e.g. 'Claude', 'DeepL'). May be empty.

note.description: Short audit-trail note on how AI was used. Recommended when extent > 0.
